document.title: Guidelines on Medical Devices: Clinical Evaluation: A Guide for Manufacturers and Notified Bodies

sourceSummary.oneLine: European Commission MEDDEV guidance on clinical evaluation of medical devices for manufacturers and notified bodies.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used to guide manufacturers, notified bodies, regulators, and stakeholders on conducting, documenting, and assessing clinical evaluation as part of conformity assessment and ongoing marketing.
keyPoints: The guidelines are legally not binding and only the text of the Directives is authentic in law.
Clinical evaluation is an ongoing process throughout the life cycle of a medical device.
Clinical evaluation includes identifying clinical data, appraising data, analysing data, and documenting conclusions in a clinical evaluation report.
The guidance applies to medical devices generally and the device component of combination products, but not in vitro diagnostics.
Notified bodies assess clinical evaluation data depending on device classification and conformity assessment route.

entities.evidence: EUROPEAN COMMISSION ENTERPRISE AND INDUSTRY DIRECTORATE GENERAL

entities.name: Enterprise and Industry Directorate General
entities.evidence: ENTERPRISE AND INDUSTRY DIRECTORATE GENERAL Consumer Goods Cosmetics and Medical Devices

entities.name: Manufacturers
entities.evidence: A GUIDE FOR MANUFACTURERS AND NOTIFIED BODIES

entities.name: Notified Bodies
entities.evidence: The Notified Body plays a key role in the assessment and verification of clinical evaluations provided by medical device manufacturers

entities.name: National Competent Authorities
entities.evidence: National Competent Authorities charged with safeguarding public health

entities.name: Regulatory Authorities
entities.evidence: communicate with conformity assessment bodies and Regulatory Authorities in accordance with local reporting requirements

entities.name: Global Harmonisation Task Force
entities.evidence: This document has been drafted on the basis of GHTF Guideline SG5/N2R8:2007 Clinical Evaluation

entities.name: EMEA
entities.evidence: a scientific opinion from a medicinal competent authority or from the EMEA

concepts.name: Clinical evaluation
concepts.evidence: Clinical evaluation is the assessment and analysis of clinical data pertaining to a medical device in order to verify the clinical safety and performance of the device.

concepts.name: Clinical evidence
concepts.evidence: The clinical evaluation report and the clinical data on which it is based serve as the clinical evidence that supports the marketing of the device.

concepts.name: Clinical evaluation report
concepts.evidence: The results of this process are documented in a clinical evaluation report.

concepts.name: Literature search
concepts.evidence: Literature searching can be used to identify published clinical data that is not in the possession of the manufacturer

concepts.name: Clinical experience data
concepts.evidence: These types of clinical data are generated through clinical use that is outside the conduct of clinical investigations

concepts.name: Clinical investigation
concepts.evidence: Any systematic investigation or study in or on one or more human subjects, undertaken to assess the safety and/or performance of a medical device.

concepts.name: Post-market clinical follow-up
concepts.evidence: MEDDEV 2.12/2 Guidelines on post-market clinical follow up

concepts.name: Benefit-risk assessment
concepts.evidence: a manufacturer should periodically review performance, safety and the benefit-risk assessment for the device through a clinical evaluation

evidence.claim: The document is MEDDEV 2.7.1 Rev.3 dated December 2009.
The document is a clinical evaluation guide for manufacturers and notified bodies.
evidence.evidenceText: CLINICAL EVALUATION: A GUIDE FOR MANUFACTURERS AND NOTIFIED BODIES

evidence.claim: The guidelines are not legally binding.
The guidance incorporates changes introduced by Directive 2007/47/EC and was applicable as of 21 March 2010.
evidence.evidenceText: These guidelines incorporate changes introduced by Directive 2007/47/EC... and will be applicable as of 21st March 2010.

evidence.claim: The document revises an earlier MEDDEV 2.7.1 document published in April 2003.
evidence.evidenceText: This document is a revision of an earlier document published in April 2003 as MEDDEV 2.7.1

evidence.claim: The document was drafted on the basis of GHTF SG5/N2R8:2007 Clinical Evaluation.
evidence.evidenceText: This document has been drafted on the basis of GHTF Guideline SG5/N2R8:2007 Clinical Evaluation

evidence.claim: Clinical evaluation is an ongoing process throughout the life cycle of a medical device.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical evaluation is an ongoing process conducted throughout the life cycle of a medical device.

evidence.claim: The primary purpose is guidance on conducting and documenting clinical evaluation as part of conformity assessment before placing a medical device on the market and to support ongoing marketing.
evidence.evidenceText: The primary purpose of this document is to provide manufacturers and notified bodies with guidance on how to conduct and document the clinical evaluation of a medical device as part of the conformity assessment procedure prior to placing a medical device on the market as well as to support its ongoing marketing.

evidence.claim: The guidance applies generally to medical devices and the device component of combination products, but not in vitro diagnostics.
evidence.evidenceText: intended to apply to medical devices generally and the device component of combination products. It is not intended to cover in vitro diagnostics.

evidence.claim: Clinical evaluation has three stages: identification of standards and clinical data, appraisal of data, and analysis of data.
evidence.evidenceText: there are three distinct stages in performing a clinical evaluation... identification of pertinent standards and clinical data; appraisal of each individual data set... and analysis of the individual data sets

evidence.claim: The clinical evaluation should be conducted by suitably qualified individuals.
evidence.evidenceText: The clinical evaluation should be conducted by a suitably qualified individual or individuals.

evidence.claim: Clinical data may come from literature search, clinical experience, or clinical investigation.
evidence.evidenceText: Identify clinical data from Literature searching &/or Clinical experience &/or Clinical investigation

evidence.claim: For implantable or class III devices, clinical investigations are required unless reliance on existing clinical data alone is duly justified.
evidence.evidenceText: for implantable or class III devices, clinical investigations are required unless it can be duly justified to rely on existing clinical data alone

evidence.claim: The clinical evaluation report should be readable as a stand-alone document by an independent party.
evidence.evidenceText: The clinical evaluation report should contain sufficient information to be read as a stand alone document by an independent party

evidence.claim: A notified body assesses and verifies clinical evaluations supporting conformity with the essential requirements of the relevant Directive.
evidence.evidenceText: The Notified Body plays a key role in the assessment and verification of clinical evaluations provided by medical device manufacturers to support demonstration of conformity of a device with the essential requirements of the relevant Directive.

evidence.claim: Demonstration of conformity with Essential Requirements must include a clinical evaluation for all classes of medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: the demonstration of conformity with Essential Requirements must include a clinical evaluation... This is applicable for all classes of medical devices.

evidence.claim: Clinical investigations by or on behalf of a manufacturer are generally expected to follow EN ISO 14155 Parts 1 and 2 or a comparable standard and local regulations.
evidence.evidenceText: Such clinical investigations are generally expected to be designed, conducted and reported in accordance with EN ISO 14155, Parts 1 and 2... or to a comparable standard, and in compliance with local regulations.

evidence.claim: ISO 14971:2007 is referenced for risk management of medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: ISO14971: 2007 Medical devices – application of risk management to medical devices.

reviewFlags.reason: No product model is identified because the source is general medical device clinical evaluation guidance rather than product-specific documentation.
Source metadata authoritySignal is general, while the document text identifies the European Commission and EU medical device directives.
Some appendix pages contain OCR or formatting artifacts, including broken table text and repeated underscores, but the core guidance text is readable.
